item.title: Bridging the gap between guidelines and practice: comparative effectiveness of ustekinumab and vedolizumab in Crohn's disease.

item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Systematic review - To evaluate the real-world effectiveness and safety of vedolizumab (VDZ) in Asian patients with IBD, including Crohn's disease (CD) and Ulcerative colitis (UC). We systematically searched PubMed, E...

item.title: Multiple switches between infliximab originator and biosimilars in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Infliximab (IFX) is highly effective but costly, driving the adoption of biosimilars. Data on switching from originator IFX to biosimilars in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients rem...

item.title: Real-World Effectiveness of Risankizumab in Refractory Crohn's Disease: The RISANCROHN Study From the ENEIDA Registry.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Risankizumab, a selective interleukin-23 p19 inhibitor, has demonstrated efficacy in clinical trials for moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease, but real-world data in highly refractory populations are...

item.title: Impact of Upadacitinib on Cardiovascular Events and Lipid Values in Older Inflammatory Bowel Disease Patients with Baseline Cardiovascular Risk.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Regulatory safety warnings for Janus kinase inhibitors, based on increased cardiovascular (CV) events in the rheumatoid arthritis populations, have raised concern regarding their use in inflammator...

item.title: Biopolymeric advanced hydrogel systems with special reference to pectin for targeted colonic drug delivery: A review.

item.title: Redox-responsive oral drug delivery systems for inflammatory bowel disease: Analytical challenges, design strategies, and translational outlook.

item.title: Comparative efficacy of ustekinumab and vedolizumab in anti-TNF-naive and anti-TNF-exposed patients with Crohn's disease: a matched cohort study.

item.title: Acute Severe Pancolitis After Initiation of a Calcitonin Gene-Related Peptide Receptor Antagonist: A Case Report.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Calcitonin gene-related peptide receptor antagonists (CGRP-RA) are increasingly prescribed for migraine prophylaxis. Gastrointestinal side effects have been described, but association with inflamma...

item.title: Dual Targeted Therapy With Risankizumab and Upadacitinib in Refractory Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Medically refractory acute severe ulcerative colitis (UC) remains a major therapeutic challenge. We describe a 39-year-old woman with multiple prior treatment failures who presented with a steroid-...

item.title: Practical considerations for empirical Bayes estimation using a pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic model developed from summary-level data of ustekinumab therapy in Crohn's disease.

item.title: Pro-inflammatory intestinal Th17-cells are tissue-resident, accumulate in the epithelia in Crohn's disease, and predict unresponsiveness to vedolizumab.

item.title: Effectiveness and safety of mirikizumab in patients with moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis: the miri real multicenter study.

item.title: Clinical outcomes of upadacitinib dose escalation in inflammatory bowel disease: A single-center retrospective cohort study.

item.title: Risk of symptomatic leishmaniasis in patients with inflammatory bowel disease receiving biologic therapy in an endemic Mediterranean region: a population-based case-control study.

item.title: Long-term treatment outcomes of upadacitinib for Crohn's disease: a single-center retrospective study.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - Upadacitinib is the sole Janus kinase inhibitor approved for Crohn's disease in Japan, with its short-term efficacy already known. However, its long-term real-world outcomes remain unclear. This st...

item.title: A multicentered retrospective cohort study comparing JAK inhibitor therapies in moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis.
